title: A moment of appreciation for dominant Red Sox ace Sonny Gray
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray, Boston Red Sox pitcher, holds a 10-1 record with a 2.61 ERA, leading the team's playoff push.
Despite an impressive season, Gray was snubbed from the All-Star roster, with teammate Ranger Suárez selected instead.
The Red Sox have improved to 13-5 recently, entering playoff contention with Gray's consistent pitching performances.

title: Lindsay Clancy’s strangulation charges dropped ahead of murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Prosecutors dropped Lindsay Clancy's strangulation charges, considering them redundant before her first-degree murder trial begins July 20.
Lindsay Clancy faces first-degree murder charges for allegedly killing her three children on January 24, 2023, in Duxbury.
Clancy's defense plans to argue insanity, citing postpartum mental illness and medication issues affecting her mental health.

title: A modern 3-bedroom condo in Newton — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 3-bedroom condo in Newton Highlands was built in 2026, totaling 2,100 square feet.
The condo includes 2 full baths, 1 half bath, and has an estimated annual tax of $15,455.
Monthly fees for the condo are $308, and pets are allowed on the premises.

title: Braintree man who fell ill, had legs amputated while visiting India for brother’s wedding has died
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Andrew Wasil, 39, from Braintree, died on June 19 after severe illness in India led to leg amputations.
Wasil contracted a dangerous strep infection in Mumbai post-wedding, resulting in septic shock and medical repatriation.
He was a 2005 Braintree High alum and longtime Stop & Shop employee, remembered for his resilience and kindness.

title: Review & setlist: From tearjerkers to ‘Brick House,’ Lionel Richie reminded us why he is iconic
author: Chris Stevens
bullets: Lionel Richie performed at TD Garden on July 8, 2026, captivating a crowd of 20,000 with hits spanning his career.
Earth, Wind & Fire opened the concert, delivering iconic songs like 'September' and 'Boogie Wonderland' to enthusiastic fans.
Richie addressed his prior health issue, jokingly advocating electrolytes, and performed a virtual duet with the late Kenny Rogers.

title: Questions of age and experience dominate first debate between Markey and Moulton
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and Rep. Seth Moulton debated in Chicopee, focusing on age and ability against the Trump administration.
Markey supports 'Medicare for all' while Moulton prefers 'Medicare for all who want it.', indicating slight policy differences.
Moulton criticized Markey's Washington tenure, advocating for fresh approaches against the Democratic 'establishment.'

title: 3 New England hotels just ranked among the 100 best in the world
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: XV Beacon in Boston ranked No. 55 in Travel + Leisure's World's Best Awards, and as the top city hotel in the continental U.S.
Quisisana Resort in Lovell, Maine, ranked No. 56 globally, and is the No. 1 resort in New England, No. 5 in the continental U.S.
The Norumbega in Camden, Maine, ranked No. 99 worldwide and is the second best inn in the continental U.S. by Travel + Leisure.

title: Man dies in apparent drowning at Plymouth pond
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 52-year-old man died in an apparent drowning at Charge Pond in Myles Standish State Forest, Plymouth.
Plymouth police and emergency services responded to a 911 call about the incident at 2 p.m. Wednesday.
The man's body was found 17 to 19 feet underwater and pronounced dead; investigation into his death continues.

title: NBA insider: Teams received ‘intel’ that steered them away from bidding on Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
Zach Lowe suggests some teams were deterred from bidding on Brown due to intel on his preferences.
Celtics aimed to avoid financial strain and maintain competitiveness by trading Brown, according to Brad Stevens.

title: ‘Let us in’ chants echo outside Brigham and Women’s Hospital as nurses strike becomes lockout
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Brigham and Women's Hospital nurses were locked out Thursday due to replacement staffing contracts after a one-day strike.
The nurses will continue picketing until July 13, when the replacement nurses' contracts are set to expire.
The labor dispute centers on wages, with the union seeking higher pay and better health insurance amid rising living costs.
